title: ROAMM
description: ROAMM is a large-scale multimodal neuroimaging dataset combining simultaneous EEG and eye-tracking recordings from 44 participants during naturalistic reading of standardized articles. The dataset includes span-level mind-wandering annotations, reading comprehension assessments, and linguistic stimulus information, providing a benchmark resource for mind-wandering detection and EEG-based language decoding in naturalistic contexts.
methods_description: Data were collected using BioSemi ActiveTwo 64-channel EEG and SR Research EyeLink 1000 Plus eye-tracking during a naturalistic reading task with retrospective self-report (ReMind task) for mind-wandering annotation. Participants read standardized articles while EEG and eye-tracking data were simultaneously recorded and synchronized.
